sections.title: The One Rule to Remember
sections.description: Most tests in I Must Kill use one simple mechanic:
sections.coreRule: Roll 1d10. Roll equal to or under your stat to succeed.
sections.coreRuleNote: A roll of 1 is always a critical success. A roll of 10 always fails.
sections.body: That's it. Every action—attacking, dodging, casting spells, tracking monsters—uses this same roll.
sections.d10Note: A d10 is a 10-sided die numbered 1–10. I Must Kill typically calls for 4 or fewer at a time — but if you only have one, just roll it multiple times.

sections.title: The Four Stats
sections.description: Every hunter has four stats, ranging from 2 to 9:
sections.quickStart: Use the balanced array (6, 6, 6, 6) for your first character.

stats.name: Body
stats.desc: Strength & endurance. Used to Brace against attacks.

stats.name: Agility
stats.desc: Speed & reflexes. Used to Dodge and Flee.

stats.name: Focus
stats.desc: Mental power. Used to cast Powers and negotiate.

stats.name: Fate
stats.desc: Luck & life force. Determines your Hit Points.

sections.title: Combat in 60 Seconds
sections.description: Combat is fast and deadly. Each round:
turnOrder: GM announces who is in danger

actions.effect: Draw a card from your Power deck

sections.title: Your First Hunt
sections.description: A typical hunt follows this structure. Use this as a template for your first session:

phases.name: The Hook
phases.desc: A desperate villager approaches. Livestock have been disappearing. They offer payment to deal with the threat.

phases.name: Negotiate Pay
phases.desc: Roll Focus to bargain. Success means good pay and spending money later. Failure means you're working for scraps.

phases.name: Gather Rumors
phases.desc: Talk to locals. The GM provides 3-4 rumors about the monster—some true, some misleading.

phases.name: Research
phases.desc: Roll Fate to learn a monster weakness. Success reveals something useful: 'It fears fire' or 'Silver burns it.'

phases.name: Days of Preparation
phases.desc: Spend time in town interacting with NPCs and locations. Succeed at skill challenges to earn Preparation Resources (Alchemy, Witchery, or Smithery). Collect 3 of a type to unlock a boon for the hunt.

phases.name: Track the Monster
phases.desc: Roll Fate to find the creature's lair. Failure means it finds you first—ambush!

phases.name: The Fight
phases.desc: Combat! Use your preparation and tactics. Exploit weaknesses. Don't die.

phases.name: Collect Your Bounty
phases.desc: Claim your pay. Low pay lets you resupply up to 5 low-tier items. High pay also grants 1 high-tier item — including Trick Weapons. Rest up and level up. Tomorrow brings another hunt.
